aliases: implicit capture from a finished session
blurb: Where memories come from: the explicit lesson tee and the agentic post-session distiller with its importance gate.
intent: Explain the two ways a memory is born and why distillation is LLM-only by contract: the explicit path (`send_to_user(type=lesson)` teed into the store by `post_tool_trace._remember_lesson` with span/agent/scope provenance, honoring a `supersedes` id), and the implicit agentic distiller (`distill.py::distill_session`) that reads a finished session's trace, is handed only the high-signal hints (`_grade_digest` grader findings + `_signal_digest` failure→fix / pushback chains) plus read-only trace tools and self-fetches just the spans it needs so prompt size stays constant. Covers the self-scored importance band (`_finalize_status`: drop below `distill_min_importance`, auto-approve at/above `auto_approve_importance`, else queue as `proposed`), dedup-at-write reinforcement (`_dedup_candidate`), at-write contradiction supersede (`_supersede_candidate`, gated by `distill_supersede_on_conflict`), meta-root auto-filing (`_link_meta_root`), and the `DISTILL_TAG` idempotency guard.
label: Memory capture & distillation (send_to_user lesson tee + agentic post-session distiller)
